cluster_name: US-Iran ceasefire collapses amid mutual strikes over Strait of Hormuz
event_summary: The US struck approximately 90 Iranian military targets on July 8–9 after Iranian forces attacked three commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz; Iran retaliated by striking US military bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Qatar, with 14 people killed and 78 injured per Iran's Health Ministry.
tldr: The headline layer presents two near-universal storylines running in parallel across 33 of 34 country buckets: the Strait of Hormuz military exchange with Trump's 'ceasefire is over' declaration, and the simultaneous Khamenei funeral procession through Iraq toward Mashhad. Iran state media (IRNA, Tehran Times) is the sharpest editorial outlier — every one of its headlines is ceremonial or diplomatic, with zero acknowledgment of concurrent US strikes on Iranian territory visible in every other outlet's titles. Italy's ANSA is the most off-topic presence in the corpus, contributing headlines about Trump's NATO comments on European troop withdrawal and a Supreme Court petition on birthright citizenship — entirely disconnected from both the strikes and the funeral. Russia's RT leads with a sensational protest headline ('Kill Trump' banner at the funeral procession) while its TASS sibling clinically reports CENTCOM strike counts without editorial framing, the two outlets occupying opposite rhetorical registers on the same conflict.

evidence.quote: more strikes after Iran's attacks in Strait of Hormuz
US, Iran launch more attacks as mediators urge
US-Iran ceasefire collapse all about control of Hormuz
Trump says interim accord with Iran to end war is 'over'
Trump Declares Iran Ceasefire 'Over' at NATO Summit

frames.sub_frame: Khamenei funeral procession through Iraq to Mashhad

evidence.quote: Coffin of Iran's slain supreme leader arrives in Mashhad
A bitterly divided Iran grapples with Khamenei's legacy
The Funeral of Power: The Message of a Historic Farewell

frames.sub_frame: Oil prices surge, tankers withdraw from Hormuz

evidence.quote: Four Oil and Gas Tankers Withdraw from Strait of Hormuz
Oil prices surge as US and Iran return to hostilities

frames.sub_frame: 14 killed, 78 injured in two days of US strikes

evidence.quote: 14 Killed, 78 Injured In US Attacks Across Iran
14 killed in two days of US strikes

silences.what_they_covered_instead: All four Iran State headlines are ceremonial or diplomatic — IRNA highlighted Pezeshkian praising Iran-Pakistan bonds and the VP calling the Karbala ceremonies a testament to 'brotherhood'; Tehran Times framed the funeral as 'The Message of a Historic Farewell.' No headline acknowledges concurrent US strikes on five Iranian provinces visible in every other outlet.

ANSA carried Trump's NATO side-comment linking US troop withdrawal from Europe to Greenland and Iran, and Trump's announcement of a Supreme Court petition on birthright citizenship — neither the strikes nor the funeral appeared in Italian headlines.

single_outlet_findings.finding: RT leads its Iran-war coverage with "'Kill Trump' banner seen in Khamenei funeral procession (VIDEOS)" — the only headline in the corpus to foreground anti-US protest chanting as the primary angle, while omitting the military exchange entirely in both of Russia's RT headlines.

"Trump Strikes Iran and Threatens War Crimes—Again" is the sole Western outlet to embed a legal/criminal accusation ('War Crimes') directly in its headline, a register absent from every other title including other progressive US outlets.

"Iran ceasefire, now 'over', was always going to break" is the only headline framing the collapse as structurally predetermined — retrospective structural analysis at the title level, a framing absent from all breaking-news headlines on the same event.

"The Funeral of Power: The Message of a Historic Farewell" is the most analytically self-aware Iran State headline: it explicitly frames the funeral as a geopolitical performance ('power,' 'messages') while simultaneously refusing to name the US strikes providing the political context.

"'This Is Punishment': US Says Latest Iran Offensive Is 4-5 Times Bigger Than Last" is the only headline to quote the US military's own 'punishment' framing verbatim, foregrounding a retributive register that appears only in body text across all other outlets.

bottom_line: At the headline layer, the US-Iran Hormuz strike exchange dominates 33 of 34 country buckets simultaneously with Khamenei's funeral procession — the two largest concurrent news events pulling editorial resources in opposite directions. Iran state media's choice to suppress all strike coverage while framing the funeral as historic 'power projection,' and Italy's ANSA contributing entirely off-topic NATO side-commentary, are the two clearest framing anomalies detectable at the title layer alone.
